cover.subtitle: Western Living finalist. 20 projects documented. What’s missing: video, discoverability, and the momentum to keep the awards coming.

story.headline_html:
The portfolio is beautiful.
Google can’t see it.
story.lead: Lauren, I ran a full technical audit on lrdstudio.ca before our coffee this week. Your site scored 84/100 on content and 85/100 on visual assets. 20 project pages, professional photography from our sessions, and a clear design voice. It’s a strong foundation.
paragraphs: But there’s a problem hiding behind that portfolio: 699 of your 761 images have no alt text. That means 91.8% of LRD’s photography is invisible to Google Image search. When a homeowner in Whistler searches “modern kitchen interior design” or “log home renovation interiors,” Google shows image results. LRD can’t appear in any of them.

You were a 2022 Western Living Designer of the Year finalist. That’s a real credential. But since then, there’s been no video content, no new award submissions, and 699 images Google can’t see. Ashley de Boer in Squamish just racked up Georgie, HAVAN, and NKBA nominations for 2025. The design quality isn’t the gap. The ongoing visibility is.

This proposal puts a creative partner behind LRD who already knows your aesthetic, captures every project with intent, builds the discoverability layer your portfolio is missing, and keeps the awards pipeline active.

audit.headline_html:
What the data says.
Verified, not estimated.
audit.lead: I crawled 30 pages on lrdstudio.ca. Here’s the real picture.

findings: SEO scores 77/100. Valid SSL, 91ms response, 36-URL sitemap, good meta title including Whistler and Squamish locations.
Content scores 84/100. 20 project pages with an average of 34.8 images each. 464-word homepage. Strong design philosophy articulation.
Professional photography. Images from our Sunridge and other sessions. Design-intent photography that shows material choices, light, and spatial composition.
Houzz listing active. Important directory presence for interior designers.
Western Living DOTY finalist (2022). Real credential. Also featured in Build Magazine. IDIBC registered with board seat. The foundation for awards is already proven.

699 of 761 images missing alt text (91.8%). This is the single biggest issue. Every image without alt text is invisible to Google Image search, which is how homeowners discover interior designers. One-time fix with permanent impact.
No Google Business Profile. Can’t appear on Google Maps or in the Local Pack for “interior designer Squamish” or “interior design Whistler.”

No video content. Zero atmospheric walkthroughs, zero material reveals, zero design process content. For interior design, video showing how light moves through a space is the most compelling content format.

Awards momentum has stalled since 2022. The Western Living finalist credential proved the work qualifies. But no new submissions since then. Ashley de Boer has 5+ nominations for 2025 alone. The pipeline needs to be reactivated.
No blog or design journal. No content targeting “interior design Whistler” or thought leadership around materials, trends, or design process.

insight.title: The Bottom Line
insight.content_html: LRD’s portfolio is genuinely beautiful and the Western Living credential proves the work is award-calibre. The issues are discoverability (699 images Google can’t see, no local presence on Maps) and momentum (no new submissions since 2022, no video content). A creative partner fixes both while keeping the content pipeline flowing.

services.intro: Photography that captures how a space feels, not just how it looks. Video that shows how light enters a room. Material storytelling that communicates design decisions. Everything delivered with SEO-ready alt text so Google can actually find it.

insight.title: How to Think About This
insight.content_html: You paid $3,150 for two sessions last year. Here’s what a monthly retainer delivers:

breakdowns.label: Foundation $2,000
breakdowns.desc: 20-25 design-intent images with proper alt text every month. Pure content creation at $850 less than a single session.

breakdowns.label: Growth $2,500
breakdowns.desc: Everything above plus atmospheric video, social reels, strategy calls, and a year-end film. The content engine starts compounding.

breakdowns.label: Creative Partner $3,500
breakdowns.desc: Content + strategy. Documentary film, IDIBC and Georgie submissions, publication pitches, and full social management.

insight.footer_html:
Every image delivered with keyword-rich alt text means your portfolio starts appearing in Google Image search from day one.

Add-On: Website + Local SEO Package

Your audit scored 46/100 on local SEO. 699 images with no alt text, no Google Business Profile, no schema markup. The fix isn’t patches on the existing site. It’s a full website rebuild to modern standards with SEO baked in from the ground up.

  • Full custom Squarespace website rebuild
  • Google Business Profile setup and optimization
  • InteriorDesigner + LocalBusiness schema markup
  • SEO-optimized project pages with proper alt text on every image
  • Service area pages for Whistler, Squamish, Pemberton, Vancouver
  • Monthly SEO blog post + ongoing site maintenance
  • Google review generation strategy

$750 to $1,250/mo added to any tier. Happy to scope this over coffee.

roadmap.intro: The pilot runs May through July. Three months, three projects documented with photography and atmospheric video.

entries: Full-day shoot at your strongest recently completed project
20 to 25 edited images: spaces, materials, light, composition

All images with keyword-rich SEO alt text

20 to 25 images: material selections, install progress, before/after moments
Design process documentation that clients and followers want to see
Social media reel from shoot content
Strategy call: review first month, adjust approach
Full-day hero session in peak summer light

Atmospheric walkthrough video
Social media reel + Pinterest content
Pilot review: what worked, what the data says, what comes next
If results proven: 12-month continuation locked in

vision.headline_html:
What a full year of
content looks like.
vision.intro: LRD’s portfolio is already beautiful. A full year of atmospheric video, design documentation, and awards submissions turns it into the most visible interior design studio in the Sea-to-Sky.

quarters.title: Prove the Model
quarters.description: 3 shoots. 75 images. 3 atmospheric videos. Design process documentation. Every image searchable by Google for the first time. Social channels active with professional content.

quarters.title: Compound
quarters.description: Peak season. Summer light in Whistler and Squamish spaces. 150+ images. Atmospheric walkthroughs banked. Design journal content driving organic traffic.

quarters.title: Leverage
quarters.description: IDIBC and Georgie award submissions. Publication pitches to Western Living and Dwell. 225+ images. LRD’s online presence matches the calibre of the work.

quarters.title: Separate
quarters.description: 300+ images, a year-end documentary, and the most visible interior design portfolio in the Sea-to-Sky. When a homeowner in Whistler searches for a designer, LRD shows up first.

awards.headline_html:
One finalist credential.
Time to build on it.
awards.lead: Lauren, the 2022 Western Living Designer of the Year finalist nod proved LRD’s work belongs at the top. But awards are a momentum game. Ashley de Boer has 5+ nominations for 2025 alone. The studios that submit consistently are the ones editors call first.
paragraphs: The Western Living credential opened the door. Consistent submissions to IDIBC, Georgie, and HAVAN keep it open. Every project photographed to award spec is another entry. The goal isn’t one finalist badge, it’s a track record.
awards.detail_html:
What I Handle

Every shoot is photographed to award-submission spec from day one. 300 DPI JPEG, colour-calibrated, anonymous-ready. When submission season opens, your images are done. I prepare entry materials, identify the right categories, and manage the process. You focus on designing.

insight.title: 20 Projects, 20 Potential Submissions
insight.content_html: LRD has the portfolio and the first credential. Sunridge alone could be submitted to IDIBC, Georgie, and HAVAN. The difference between one finalist nod and five is consistency, not quality. Keep the pipeline active.

next_steps.headline_html:
We already know
how we work together.
paragraphs: Lauren, we’ve done two sessions and the images speak for themselves. This is the next step: consistent content so every project LRD designs starts working for the studio’s visibility the moment it’s complete.

The pilot is three months. If it works, we keep going. If it doesn’t, you walk away with 75 professional images (all with proper alt text), atmospheric video of your spaces, three social reels, and a portfolio that’s finally visible to Google. Plus a reactivated awards pipeline. There’s no downside.
